case_title: RYAN PARK VS. DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES
cause_of_action: WRITS OF MANDATE OR PROH., CERTI., ETC./ADMIN. AGEN

docket_entries.description: NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITION FOR WRIT IOF MANDATE AND/OR PROHIBITION OR OTHER APPROPRIATE RELIEF, PROOF OF SERVICE, DECLARATION FILED BY PETITIONER PARK, RYAN HEARING SET FOR SEP-10-2008 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
MINI-MINUTES FOR SEP-10-2008 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
MINI-MINUTES FOR SEP-30-2008 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED DENYING PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE FILED BY RESPONDENT DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES
ANSWER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION FILED BY RESPONDENT DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES
MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES OPPOSING PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE FILED BY DEFENDANT DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER RYAN PARK'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE AND/OR PROHIBITION OR OTHER APPROPRIATE RELIEF. THE WRIT IS DENIED. THE DECISION IN FEARN V. ZOLIN (1992) 9 CAL. APP. 4TH 1756, MAKES CLEAR THAT THE DMV IS RESPONSIBLE FOR SUSPENDING PLAINTIFF'S LICENSE BASED ON THE CRIMINAL CONVICTION AND THE ADMINISTRATIVE PROCEEDING. THE CRIMINAL SUSPENSION DOES NOT RELATE BACK TO PLAINTIFF'S RELEASE ON BAIL BUT BEGINS ONLY AFTER THE CONVICTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
ORDER DENYING PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE
PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION FILED BY PETITIONER PARK, RYAN AS TO RESPONDENT DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER RYAN PARK'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE AND/OR PROHIBITION OR OTHER APPROPRIATE RELIEF IS CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 30, 2008. BOTH PARTIES ARE TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302 WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Iof Mandate And/Or Prohibition Or Other Appropriate Relief
Off Calendar Sep-30-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Paul Alaga (581.0885) for petitioner and moving party
David Carrillo of the Office of the Attorney General (510.622.2215) for respondent and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PETITIONER RYAN PARK'S PETITION FOR WRIT IOF MANDATE AND/OR PROHIBITION OR OTHER APPROPRIATE RELIEF. The Writ is denied. The decision in Fearn v. Zolin (1992) 9 Cal. App. 4th 1756, makes clear that the DMV is responsible for suspending plaintiff's license based on the criminal conviction and the administrative proceeding. The criminal suspension does not relate back to plaintiff's release on bail but begins only after the conviction. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/JW) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Iof Mandate And/Or Prohibition Or Other Appropriate Relief
Off Calendar Sep-10-2008 Continued To Sep-30-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. PETITIONER RYAN PARK'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE AND/OR PROHIBITION OR OTHER APPROPRIATE RELIEF is continued to September 30, 2008. Both parties are to provide courtesy copies of their papers to Department 302 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. (302/PJM) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
